bullets.text: Q1 CR 89.3 with NPW growth 19%; all segments profitable except Property (weather 30.6 CR points); Commercial Lines CR 83.9 (+57% NPW) driven by freight market boom and pandemic recovery.

Companywide PIFs up 12% YOY, crossing 25 million milestone; personal auto and special lines surpassed 17M and 5M PIFs respectively — first time all three product lines grew double digits simultaneously since June 2004.

NPW growth 19% YOY; March 17 was second highest Direct auto prospect/sales day ever, boosted by third round of stimulus payments; commercial auto new business hit all-time highs in late Q1 and early April.

Auto model 8.6 in 32 states (59% of premium); model 8.7 launched in January 2021; commercial model 8.0 with new policy admin system in 12 states; BOP in 19 states (37% of CL multiperil market); Protective Insurance acquisition announced for ~$338M ($23.30/share).

Q1 investment return 0.3% (equity strength offset rising rates); duration increased slightly on higher yields; capital position described as strong; Protective acquisition subject to regulatory approvals expected to close in 2021.

Vehicle miles traveled down 8–10% through March vs. pre-pandemic average but rising; claims frequency increasing as states reopened; claims staffing ramping to handle anticipated volume increase through remainder of 2021.

Snapshot Apron Relief program launched in 43 states offering existing customers 30-day monitoring period to realize savings faster; single-credential login launched across Progressive auto and home online platforms; Robinson PIF growth ~20% in Direct channel.

People of color in senior leadership growing toward goal of doubling by 2025; on track for women in leadership parity by 2025; 'broken first rung' programs added to Multicultural Leadership Development program to develop supervisor-level diverse talent.

New self-service personal auto website launched for all eligible policyholders after successful testing showing higher satisfaction and better digital self-service completion; real-time data feeds integrated between auto and home products.

Commercial auto benefiting from freight market strength, intermodal bottlenecks, and supply chain disruptions driving demand; TNC recovering as Uber/Lyft rideshare mileage rebounded; business auto and contractor BMTs also strengthening.
